question: How many times through 2025 has the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine been shared by three laureates?

question: Which woman was the only one to receive an unshared Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine?

article: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine
question: Who was the first Nobel laureate in Physiology or Medicine to reach the 100th birthday?
source_sentence: Rita Levi-Montalcini, an Italian neurologist, who together with colleague Stanley Cohen and Lucas Pu, received the 1986 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for their discovery of nerve growth factor (NGF), was the first Nobel laureate to reach the 100th birthday.

article: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine
question: In what year was the provision added that no more than three persons may share a Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine?

article: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine
question: How many times has the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine not been awarded?
source_sentence: There have been nine years in which the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine was not awarded (1915–1918, 1921, 1925, 1940–1942).

article: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine
question: Who was the 2025 female Nobel laureate in Physiology or Medicine?
source_sentence: Thirteen women have received the prize: Gerty Cori (1947), Rosalyn Yalow (1977), Barbara McClintock (1983), Rita Levi-Montalcini (1986), Gertrude B. Elion (1988), Christiane Nüsslein-Volhard (1995), Linda B. Buck (2004), Françoise Barré-Sinoussi (2008), Elizabeth H. Blackburn (2009), Carol W. Greider (2009), May-Britt Moser (2014), Tu Youyou (2015), Katalin Karikó (2023) and Mary E. Brunkow (2025).
